title: Unconventional chiral charge order in kagome superconductor KV3Sb5

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports STM discovery of a robust 2×2 charge order in the kagome superconductor KV3Sb5, with a gap opening near the Fermi level and a real-space intensity reversal across the gap consistent with charge-density-wave (CDW) order. The authors further find a chiral anisotropy of the charge modulation that switches with c-axis magnetic field, and interpret it as an unconventional time-reversal-breaking chiral CDW that may underlie the material’s anomalous Hall response and relate to superconductivity.

figures.description: Crystal structure of KV3Sb5 shown in 3D and top view, highlighting the layered kagome-related structure.
Schematic of cleaving surfaces and step-edge termination assignment between K and Sb surfaces.
STM topography of a step edge showing K and Sb surfaces, with atomically resolved lattice images of each termination.
Large-area Sb-surface STM topography at 4.2 K showing a 2×2 modulation.
Large-area Sb-surface STM topography at 80 K showing disappearance of the 2×2 modulation.
Fourier transform of the 4.2 K Sb topography showing 2×2 ordering peaks and Bragg peaks.
Fourier transform of the 80 K Sb topography showing only Bragg peaks.
Spatially averaged dI/dV spectra on the Sb surface at 4.2 K and 80 K, showing a gap-like feature near the Fermi level.
First-principles calculated LDOS for the optimized 2×2 structure, with an inset showing hexamer/trimer bond modulation in the kagome lattice.
Magnetic-field-dependent dI/dV spectra up to 6 T showing little detectable field response of the gap.
Atomically resolved dI/dV map at -30 meV showing charge modulation contrast.
Atomically resolved dI/dV map at +30 meV showing contrast reversal relative to -30 meV.
Fourier transform of the -30 meV dI/dV map showing charge-order and lattice peaks.
Energy dependence of charge-order and Bragg peak intensities showing non-dispersive 2×2 modulation.
figures.data_type: line plot / Fourier-peak intensity vs energy

figures.description: Spectroscopic 2×2 Fourier peaks at zero field showing anisotropic intensities among the six ordering vectors.
Spectroscopic 2×2 Fourier peaks at -2 T showing field-dependent chirality.
Spectroscopic 2×2 Fourier peaks at +2 T showing chirality switching relative to -2 T.
Schematic low-energy Fermiology of KV3Sb5, including Sb pz pocket, V dxy van Hove band, and V dxz/dyz Dirac-like bands, with CDW wave vectors.
Model calculation of chiral CDW on the V dxy kagome bands, showing gap opening and orbital-current pattern in the 2×2 unit cell.
figures.data_type: calculated band structure / density of states

figures.description: Effective band structure of folded V dxz/dyz Dirac-like bands after 2×2 reconstruction.
Chiral CDW-induced topological gap and associated Berry-curvature-driven anomalous Hall effect schematic.
Large-area STM topographies showing typical cleaved surfaces with varying amounts of K clusters.
Atom-resolved STM topography of the Sb honeycomb surface.
Tunneling point spectra on the Sb surface from different samples.
Comparison of dI/dV spectra on K and Sb surfaces, averaged over defect-free areas.
Spectroscopic maps and Fourier transforms at different magnetic fields and energies, showing low-energy chirality switching and no switching at 50 meV.
Energy dependence of the three 2×2 vector peak intensities for three samples in defect-free regions.
Defect-rich region topography and corresponding Fourier features, plus field-dependent peak intensities showing suppressed chirality and weak field response.
DFT band structure of KV3Sb5 without spin-orbit coupling, with orbital character and irreducible representations at M.
Single-crystal synchrotron X-ray diffraction maps at 100 K and 50 K showing superlattice reflections at 50 K with propagation vector (0.5, 0.5, 0.5).

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include STM topographic scans, atom-resolved STM images, dI/dV point spectra, dI/dV spectroscopic maps, magnetic-field-dependent STM/STS spectra and maps, and synchrotron X-ray diffraction frames collected at CHESS. The paper also mentions muon spin spectroscopy results, but those data are not shown here and are said to be published elsewhere.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, or downloadable source-data statement is included in the provided text. The paper describes experimental methods and figure contents, but does not state where the underlying datasets or scripts are deposited.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data are described as STM topographies, dI/dV spectra, dI/dV maps, and X-ray diffraction detector frames. Acquisition conditions are given in Methods, including bias/current settings, lock-in frequency, oscillation amplitude, temperature, and magnetic-field protocols. File formats are not specified.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Fourier transforms of STM topography and spectroscopic maps, spatially averaged spectra over defect-free areas, energy-dependent peak-intensity traces, line cuts through diffraction planes, and extracted/visualized superlattice reflections. Theoretical processed outputs include DFT band structures, LDOS, and effective-model band structures and Berry-curvature-derived quantities.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly presented in the main text and supplement as images, spectra, Fourier-space maps, and line plots. However, the text does not explicitly say that source data files for each figure are provided as a separate dataset.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text. Theoretical calculations are described mathematically, but no executable scripts are referenced.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multimodal and mixes experimental raw measurements with derived/processed representations and theory outputs. Important processing steps include Fourier transformation of STM data, averaging spectra over defect-free regions, energy-resolved peak tracking, and reciprocal-space reconstruction of XRD data from rotation frames. The paper also distinguishes defect-free versus defect-rich regions, and field-dependent measurements are performed with specific drift compensation and re-approach procedures.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods explicitly describe acquisition of STM topography, dI/dV spectra/maps, and X-ray diffraction frames, indicating raw experimental measurements were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figures and supplement present Fourier transforms, averaged spectra, line cuts, and energy-dependent peak intensities, which are processed figure-ready data derived from the raw measurements.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: No explicit data availability statement is present in the provided text; relevant phrases are in Methods and captions such as 'Fourier transform of the Sb topographic image', 'spatially averaged dI/dV spectrums', 'energy dependence of the charge order vector', and 'line cuts ... were created summing the over three neighboring axes'.
